title: hemistegia horrida
intro: Hemistegia horrida is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, known for its small white flowers and spiny stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its alkaloids and saponins. Hemistegia horrida has been used to make herbal preparations such as teas and tinctures, which are said to have health benefits including reducing fever and promoting wound healing.
